channel.title: NYT > Arts > Television
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Has the World Cup Turned Me Into … a Sports Guy?
item.description: The pleasures of watching the TV show of the summer as a bandwagon fan.
item.dc:creator: James Poniewozik
item.media:credit: Vincent Alban for The New York Times
item.media:description: The spectacle of the World Cup, which wraps up on Sunday, stretches far beyond the pitch.

item.title: Octavia Spencer Believed in Herself Even When Hollywood Didn’t
item.description: It took years for her to be cast as “the main character,” but she considers that the industry’s loss. Her latest series is the action comedy “Ride or Die.”
item.dc:creator: Alexis Soloski and Thea Traff
item.media:credit: Thea Traff for The New York Times
item.media:description: “I wish I could bottle it up and give it to the world,” Spencer said of her self-confidence, “to say you have importance, take up as much space as you want.”

item.title: Chloe Fineman Is Leaving ‘S.N.L.’ After 7 Seasons
item.description: The comedian and actress announced her departure as the show looks ahead to its 52nd season.
item.dc:creator: Maya Salam
item.media:credit: Colin Cauldwell NBC, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Britney Spears was one of many celebrities portrayed by Chloe Fineman during her time on “Saturday Night Live.”

item.title: ‘Heartstopper Forever’: Growing Up, and Staying True to Themselves
item.description: Like “Heated Rivalry,” this beloved Netflix romance has a big-hearted ethos. It’s bringing that spirit to a new film that rounds out a teenage TV hit.
item.dc:creator: Eleanor Stanford

item.title: ‘Difficult People’ a Decade Later: A Sarcastic Time Capsule
item.description: This cult comedy about two snide show-business outsiders has a new Blu-ray edition, and a reunion this fall. The creator Julie Klausner discusses why she’d love to revive it.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Egner
item.media:description: Julie Klausner and Billy Eichner starred in “Difficult People,” which ran for three seasons on Hulu. (With Derrick Baskin, background.)

item.title: In ‘The Westies,’ Chris Brancato Expands His ‘Scumbag Universe’ (Again)
item.description: For his new series, the co-creator of “Narcos” and “Godfather of Harlem” turns his focus to a violent gang of Irish New Yorkers in the early 1980s.
item.dc:creator: Austin Considine and David Billet
item.media:credit: David Billet for The New York Times

item.title: Fans of ‘Love Island USA’ Ask: ‘Are These Couples for Real?’
item.description: For many viewers of the hit reality TV show, lively debates over whether the competing couples are truly in love is a central part of being a fan.
item.dc:creator: Julia Jacobs

item.title: Review: ‘Little House on the Prairie’ Adds On an Expansion
item.description: The classic story (and recent culture-war object) remains wholesome but makes room for people who called the prairie home before the Ingalls family.
item.dc:creator: James Poniewozik
item.category: Little House on the Prairie (2026) (TV Program)

item.title: Orna Guralnik of ‘Couples Therapy’ Considers Kendrick Lamar a Genius
item.description: Outside of her job as the therapist on the series “Couples Therapy,” she has a favorite clip of the rapper performing and shows it to everyone she knows.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Gonzalez
item.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times

item.title: ‘The Daily Show’ Tackles Trump’s Latest Move in His ‘War on Canada’
item.description: “Fighting again with Canada?” Desi Lydic said about the president imposing new tariffs on the country. “I thought we were going to work this ‘oot.’”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)

item.title: Whitney Cummings’s Trump Joke at Kennedy Center Is Cut From Netflix Special
item.description: At the Mark Twain Prize ceremony, the comedian Whitney Cummings made a joke about the president that seemed to refer to Jeffrey Epstein.
item.dc:creator: Malia Mendez
item.category: Kennedy, John F, Center for the Performing Arts
item.media:description: Whitney Cummings opened the ceremony for the Mark Twain Prize, which was awarded to Bill Maher at the Kennedy Center last month.

item.title: Bill Maher Feuded With Trump. Did He Get the Last Laugh?
item.description: How the late-night host won comedy’s biggest prize — despite the White House’s objections.
item.dc:creator: Benoit Denizet-Lewis
item.category: Kennedy, John F, Center for the Performing Arts
Real Time With Bill Maher (TV Program)
item.media:credit: Emily Shur for The New York Times

item.title: Cristo Fernandez Talks Returning to Pro Soccer After “Ted Lasso”
item.description: Cristo Fernandez was a soccer player before he was an actor. After finding TV stardom, he’s making the unlikely return to his first career.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Abrams
item.media:credit: Paul Ratje for The New York Times
item.media:description: Cristo Fernandez recently made his first appearance for El Paso Locomotive FC in the second tier of men’s professional soccer in the United States.

item.title: Seth Meyers Calls Trump ‘The Sports Reaper’
item.description: The late-night host joked that the president’s sports curse had struck again after he backed Lionel Messi of Argentina, which then lost the World Cup final to Spain.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)
The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)

item.title: ‘Heated Rivalry,’ ‘Dukes,’ With Jane Wickline, and More Must-See Theater
item.description: As the summer heats up, our critics are raving about these seven not-to-miss Off Broadway shows.
item.dc:creator: Helen Shaw and Elisabeth Vincentelli
item.category: Heated Rivalry: The Unauthorized Musical Parody (Play)
Goodbye Horses: An Unauthorized Retelling of Jonathan Demme's "The Silence of the Lambs" (Play)
What a World! What a World! (Play)

item.title: Cat Cohen Turned Her Stroke Into a Punchline and Off Broadway Show
item.description: “Broad Strokes” recounts this singing comedian’s medical emergency, juxtaposing “something brash and grotesque and humiliating with something completely heartfelt.”
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Gellman and Tess Mayer
item.media:description: For Cohen, there is pathos in playing silly moments off the sincere.

item.title: ‘Ransom Canyon,’ and 5 More Shows to Watch on TV This Week
item.description: The second season of the cowboy drama airs. And a documentary shows the behind the scenes of the “Heartstopper: Forever” movie.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Gonzalez
item.media:description: From left: Josh Duhamel and Jack Schumacher in “Ransom Canyon.”

item.title: ‘House of the Dragon’ Season 3, Episode 5 Recap: Under Pressure
item.description: Queen Rhaenrya is beginning to sound a little paranoid. That rarely bodes well with an autocrat.
item.dc:creator: Sean T. Collins
item.media:description: Rhaenyra (Emma D’Arcy, right) is right when she tells Daemon (Matt Smith) there is a mastermind behind the violence in the city. What’s worrisome is how she talks about it.

item.title: Hal Williams, Actor in First Wave of Black Sitcoms, Dies at 91
item.description: As a divorced father of three in Ohio, he moved to Hollywood to pursue his boyhood dream to act. He became a recognizable face on “Sanford and Son.”
item.dc:creator: Alex Traub
item.media:description: Hal Williams, second from left, as Officer “Smitty” Smith on “Sanford and Son,” the role for which he was best known. With him, from left, are Howard Platt, Redd Foxx and, in the background, Demond Wilson.

item.title: How Kerry Washington and Whitney White Are Honoring Whoopi Goldberg Through Theater
item.description: The actress Kerry Washington and the director Whitney White want to honor Goldberg’s theatrical legacy by revisiting her acclaimed portraits of humanity.
item.dc:creator: Salamishah Tillet
item.media:credit: Marcus Maddox for The New York Times
item.media:description: The director Whitney White and the actress Kerry Washington collaborated on “The Whoopi Monologues,” at Lincoln Center Theater.

item.title: Your ‘Live’ World Cup Broadcast Is Not Really Live
item.description: Everyone is watching the goals after they happen, and some get them significantly faster.
item.dc:creator: Victor Mather
item.media:description: Lautaro Martinez of Argentina scored the winning goal over England on Wednesday. When did you see it?

item.title: Lindsay Hubbard Prefers a Long-Distance Boyfriend
item.description: The “Summer House” cast member once yelled at a boyfriend, “How many sandwiches have you made for me?” These days, she’s enjoying her space.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Gonzalez

item.title: ‘Heartstopper Forever’: What to Know Before Seeing the Movie
item.description: The Netflix series and its feature-length finale are based on the graphic novels by Alice Oseman. Here’s a reminder of the events of the characters and where their stories left off.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Gonzalez

item.title: Late Night Is Amused by Alleged White House Teleprompter Grift
item.description: Hosts responded on Thursday to a teleprompter operator reportedly winning around $100,000 betting on what President Trump would say in his speeches.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)

item.title: ‘Today’ Anchor Craig Melvin Chases Intruder From 30 Rock as Show Airs
item.description: The incident did not result in any injuries, the police said. The suspect has been charged with hate crimes.
item.dc:creator: Sopan Deb
item.media:credit: Dinara Khairova/Sipa USA, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Craig Melvin on the “Today” show plaza in Midtown Manhattan last week.

item.title: Dave Kendall, Creator and Host of MTV’s ‘120 Minutes,’ Dies at 63
item.description: With his English accent and ubiquitous leather jacket, Mr. Kendall was the face of alternative music on MTV from 1989 to 1992.
item.dc:creator: John S.W. MacDonald
item.media:description: Dave Kendall at Limelight nightclub in New York in 1993. As the host of “120 Minutes” on MTV, he promoted acts like R.E.M., the Red Hot Chili Peppers and Nirvana, who became mainstream stars.

item.title: Why Netflix’s ‘The Polygamist’ Has Viewers Outside South Africa Talking
item.description: The hit Netflix show follows the life of a wealthy married couple in South Africa as they deal with the pressures of infidelity and appearances.
item.dc:creator: Zimasa Matiwane

item.title: Jimmy Fallon Pokes Fun at Joe Biden’s New Memoir
item.description: “It’s the second book Biden has written,” Fallon said of the former president. “The first was ‘The Odyssey.’”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)
The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)

item.title: Scott Bryce, ‘As the World Turns’ Actor, Dies at 68
item.description: He originated the role of the scheming Craig Montgomery and appeared in 280 episodes of the CBS soap opera over more than two decades.
item.dc:creator: Johnny Diaz
item.media:description: Scott Bryce in 2007. He originated the role of Craig Montgomery on “As the World Turns” in 1982 and played him off and on until 2008.

item.title: ‘Hadestown’ Hits Movie Theaters, and Theater to Stream in July
item.description: Streaming picks include the family-friendly sci-fi adventure “Franklin’s Key” in Philadelphia and “Hamlet” at the Delacorte Theater in Central Park.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Sherman
